attributes.description:
Sets the text color of the cancel button in the datetime picker (the button that dismisses the picker without selecting a date/time). This determines what color the cancel button text appears in.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-cancel-button-text-color

Controls the size of the cancel button text in the datetime picker. This affects how large the cancel button text appears.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-cancel-button-text-size

Controls how thick or bold the cancel button text appears in the datetime picker. 'normal' makes it regular weight, 'bold' makes it thicker, and numbers (100-900) provide fine control over thickness.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-cancel-button-text-weight

Sets the background color of the cancel button in the datetime picker. When transparent, only the button text is visible. When filled with a color, the button appears with a solid background.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-cancel-button-background

Sets the background color of the selected/confirm button in the datetime picker (the button that confirms the selected date/time). This fills the button with a color to visually indicate it's the primary action.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-selected-button-background

Sets the text color of the selected/confirm button in the datetime picker. This ensures the button text is clearly visible against the colored background.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-selected-button-text-color

Controls the size of the selected/confirm button text in the datetime picker. This affects how large the button text appears.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-selected-button-text-size

Controls how thick or bold the selected/confirm button text appears in the datetime picker. 'normal' makes it regular weight, 'bold' makes it thicker, and numbers (100-900) provide fine control over thickness.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-selected-button-text-weight

Sets the background color of the datetime picker (the modal interface where users select dates and times). This is the main background color that appears behind the picker interface.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-background

Controls the thickness of the border around datetime picker buttons. This creates the outline that defines each button shape.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-button-border-width

Controls the style of the border around datetime picker buttons. 'solid' creates a continuous line, 'dashed' creates a dotted line, 'dotted' creates small dots.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-button-border-style

Controls the corner rounding of datetime picker buttons. When set to 'pill', the buttons appear with fully rounded corners, creating a pill-shaped appearance.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-button-border-radius

Sets the color of the border around datetime picker buttons. This determines the color of the outline that defines each button shape.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-button-border-color

Sets the ripple effect color that appears when users tap datetime picker buttons. This creates a visual feedback animation to indicate user interaction. When transparent, no ripple effect is visible.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-button-ripple-color

Controls the size of the datetime picker text (the date/time values displayed in the picker). This affects how large the text appears.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-text-font-size

Controls how thick or bold the datetime picker text appears. 'normal' makes it regular weight, 'bold' makes it thicker, and numbers (100-900) provide fine control over thickness.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-text-font-weight

Sets the text color of the datetime picker (the date/time values displayed in the picker). This determines what color the text appears in, which should contrast well with the background for readability.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-text-color

Controls the size of the selected datetime picker text (the date/time value that is currently selected). This affects how large the selected text appears.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-selected-text-font-size

Controls how thick or bold the selected datetime picker text appears. 'normal' makes it regular weight, 'bold' makes it thicker, and numbers (100-900) provide fine control over thickness.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-selected-text-font-weight

Sets the text color of the selected datetime picker value (the date/time that is currently selected). This determines what color the selected text appears in to distinguish it from unselected values.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-selected-text-color

Controls the size of the datetime picker header text (the title that appears at the top of the picker interface). This affects how large the header text appears.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-header-text-font-size

Controls how thick or bold the datetime picker header text appears. When set to 700, the header text appears bold to emphasize the picker title.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-header-text-font-weight

Sets the text color of the datetime picker header (the title that appears at the top of the picker interface). This determines what color the header text appears in.
CSS variable: --wm-datetime-header-text-color
